title: podman cp via STDIN leaves temporary files at tmpdir
body:
### Issue Description

When a file is copied to a running container with `podman cp` by streaming a tar archive from STDIN, a temporary file is created https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/bde942e21622d489125e3e190988b342e4e7d852/cmd/podman/containers/cp.go#L381
This file is not deleted when the operation is completed causing tmpdir to fills up eventually.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start a test container
```
$ podman run -dt -p 8080:80/tcp docker.io/library/httpd
67c085b8f99fbbe72c86c8d6077d586627c8521858e247a2017a5aaf3cec9d09
```
2. Create a tar.gz file and copy it into the test container
```
$ ls -l ls.tar.gz
-rw-rw-r--. 1 centos centos 66545 Aug  3 12:09 ls.tar.gz
$ podman cp - 67c085b8f99f:/ < ls.tar.gz
```
3. When the operation is finished, temporary file is still present at tempdir
```
$ ls -l /var/tmp
total 340
-rw-------. 1 centos centos 66545 Aug  3 12:11 2002620236
-rw-------. 1 centos centos 66545 Aug  3 12:11 2711454316
-rw-------. 1 centos centos 66545 Aug  3 12:11 3695615676
-rw-------. 1 centos centos 66545 Aug  3 12:11 552526117
-rw-------. 1 centos centos 66545 Aug  3 12:11 623461537
```


### Describe the results you received

Garbage files are present at tempdir
```
$ ls -l /var/tmp
total 340
-rw-------. 1 centos centos 66545 Aug  3 12:11 2002620236
-rw-------. 1 centos centos 66545 Aug  3 12:11 2711454316
-rw-------. 1 centos centos 66545 Aug  3 12:11 3695615676
-rw-------. 1 centos centos 66545 Aug  3 12:11 552526117
-rw-------. 1 centos centos 66545 Aug  3 12:11 623461537
```

### Describe the results you expected

I'd expect podman to delete the temporary files when copy operation is finished.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Thanks for the detailed report, are you interested in opening a PR to fix it?

Hi,
Is there any ETA for this fix to be released ?

Thanks.
Podman 4.7 is scheduled to be released in October.

Hi,

Can you please confirm if this fix was released in 4.7.X ?

I see: 
`Temporary files created when dealing with images in /var/tmp will automatically be cleaned up on reboot.`
Does it mean, that users need to restart the podman for cleanup ?

Thank you.
They should be cleaned up right away not at reboot.
